subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, AMRO, EIA, Freedom House, RSF, TI, HRW, ICJ, Minority Rights Group) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.

A wealthy oil-and-gas absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah, one of the world's longest-reigning) with no political freedoms, a near-non-existent free press, and a 2014-19 sharia penal code (stoning provisions) held in check only by a revocable death-penalty moratorium.

Standout features: high wealth, no income tax, cradle-to-grave welfare, sound peg, and low crime, against extreme hydrocarbon mono-dependence (declining output, a large fiscal deficit), catastrophic civil liberties, and ~20,000+ excluded stateless residents.

economic.notes: Huge sovereign buffers and a stable peg, against extreme hydrocarbon mono-dependence and a structural deficit.

fiscal_state.notes: Huge sovereign buffers (the BIA fund) and no public debt, but a structural, oil-price-dependent fiscal deficit (~11.5-13% of GDP projected 2026) as output declines. (IMF 2025 Art IV; AMRO)

monetary_independence.notes: The Brunei dollar in a currency-interchangeability/board arrangement at par with the Singapore dollar - low independence but exceptionally stable. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The Brunei dollar is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Very low/near-zero, anchored to the Singapore peg. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A small, well-capitalised, conservatively-run, Islamic-finance-oriented system - limited but sound. (IMF; State Dept 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Massive single-commodity concentration (hydrocarbons ~90% of exports) with declining reserves and slow diversification. (EIA; IMF; State Dept 2025)

institutional.notes: A stable, predictable but unchecked absolute monarchy with a sharia-code arbitrariness risk and no free press.

rule_of_law.notes: Stable and predictable with a moderate CPI (~63), but power is unchecked (absolute monarchy) and the sharia code introduces severe arbitrariness. (TI CPI 2024; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Adequate basic macro data but limited transparency, especially around the sovereign fund. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Well-resourced and functional, though patronage-laden and oversized relative to the private sector. (State Dept 2025)

press_freedom.notes: 'Virtually non-existent' - media owned by the Sultan's family, pervasive self-censorship, and prison terms for 'malicious' content. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: Highly stable under one ruler but with no freedoms, a sharia code, and an excluded stateless minority.

political_stability.notes: Highly stable under one of the world's longest-reigning monarchs, but resting on a single unaccountable ruler (key-person/succession risk). (Freedom House 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: No elected national representation; assembly/expression/religion heavily restricted, and the sharia code criminalises same-sex relations and extramarital sex. (Freedom House 2025; HRW; ICJ)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: ~20,000+ stateless (mostly multi-generational ethnic Chinese) barred from subsidised healthcare, higher education, and property. (Minority Rights Group; UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: A generally calm Malay-Muslim-majority society, with the structural Chinese-minority/statelessness fault line. (Minority Rights Group)

demographics.notes: Below-replacement fertility (~1.8) but a still-growing population buoyed by foreign labour, with no acute near-term pressure. (Demographics data)

geopolitical.notes: A quiet ASEAN hedger and South China Sea claimant that avoids confrontation, with an open account.

alliance_reliability.notes: A quiet, consistent ASEAN member hedging between China, the US, Singapore, and Australia. (State Dept 2025)

conflict_involvement.notes: A South China Sea claimant that deliberately stays quiet and avoids confrontation. (State Dept 2025)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account, unsanctioned, with free convertibility under the Singapore arrangement. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Top-tier (citizen) healthcare, modern infrastructure, and very low crime, against modest climate exposure.

climate.notes: Wealthy, forested, low-population, with rising coastal/heat exposure but a strong fiscal buffer. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: A free, high-quality universal system for citizens - among Asia's finest - though excluding stateless residents. (Minority Rights Group)

infrastructure.notes: Modern, well-funded infrastructure and utilities. (State Dept 2025)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime, one of the safest countries in the region. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: State fiscal buffers effectively self-insure; the commercial market is thin but exposure is modest. (inference)

summary: A wealthy, stable, low-crime absolute monarchy that delivers genuine material comfort - no income tax, cradle-to-grave welfare, top-tier healthcare for citizens, and a sound peg to the Singapore dollar. But it is structurally fragile in two ways: an economy almost wholly dependent on declining oil and gas (~90% of exports, with a large and growing fiscal deficit), and an absence of political freedom - no elections, an essentially non-existent free press, and a 2014-19 sharia penal code with stoning provisions that only a revocable moratorium holds in check. Stateless ethnic-Chinese residents are systematically excluded from the welfare bargain. The skew is negative: stability and wealth are real but rest on a single ruler and a depleting resource, with civil liberties scoring catastrophically.
